expectations.evidence: Critique references specific JSX elements: 'ROWS' and 'COLS' static arrays, 'handleCreate', 'RULE_COLORS' hardcoded hex values, 'onMouseEnter'/'onMouseLeave' inline style mutations, 'RefreshIc'/'ColIc' SVG components, and cites specific line numbers (264–268, 278).
File contains a Rigor Matrix Summary table at the end with all four dimensions (Feasibility, UX, Business Value, Product Delight) and scores.
File contains '## Verdict Up Front' at the top and '## One-Line Verdict' at the end, both containing direct verdict statements.

UX section names specific interactions: two submit paths (body 'Create User Activity Rule' button vs footer 'Create' button), missing row actions (Edit/Pause/Delete/Duplicate), hardcoded 'left: 48%' reach gauge indicator, and missing aria-label on icon-only buttons.
Business section states 'CPM rules affect revenue directly' and 'yield optimization this is a first-class concern', connecting CPM rule management directly to yield and revenue impact.
File contains '## 1. Feasibility (Technical Quality)', '## 2. UX Quality', '## 3. Business Value', and '## 4. Product Delight' as section headers, covering all four lenses.
